explanation: Spring Boot Admin is a well-known monitoring and administration tool that aggregates actuator endpoints from multiple Spring Boot services. In this architecture, it receives HTTP connections from Customer Management Backend, Customer Self-Service Backend, and Policy Management Backend. While it has a narrow, focused responsibility (monitoring/administration), it is a standard infrastructure component rather than a business microservice, so its fine-grained scope is intentional and justified. There is no strong evidence of a true Nano Service smell — no business logic service appears to be split into trivially small units. The architecture shows a reasonable decomposition into Customer, Policy, and Risk domains. The concern is mild and primarily directed at whether Spring Boot Admin warrants independent deployment in this context, but its cross-cutting monitoring role generally justifies it.
